description_long: This is a weathered stone grave monument featuring a tall, rectangular pedestal topped with a prominent Latin cross. The cross appears to be made of the same stone as the base, with visible signs of aging and discoloration. The pedestal is composed of several stacked stone blocks, with the uppermost block bearing engraved inscriptions that are partially legible. Moss and lichen growth are visible at the base, indicating long-term exposure to the elements. The monument stands on a slightly wider stone plinth, which is partially embedded in the ground. The overall appearance suggests a traditional grave marker commonly found in older cemeteries.
description: A tall, weathered stone grave monument with a Latin cross atop a multi-tiered pedestal, featuring engraved inscriptions and moss at the base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the engraved inscriptions on the upper pedestal and the full Latin cross, with moss visible at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the pedestal and the cross, with moss and lichen more prominent on the left side.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain back of the monument, with no visible inscriptions and a clear view of the stone block structure.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view showing the monument's layered construction and the moss at the base extending around the side.
